What is the best programming language for beginners?
Jane Developer
1,234 followers
John Coder Software Engineer at Google
2,345 upvotes

Python is by far the best language for beginners. Its syntax is clean and readable, making it easy to learn the fundamental concepts of programming without getting bogged down in complex syntax rules.

Here are some reasons why Python is great for beginners:

  • Simple, readable syntax
  • Large community and tons of learning resources
  • Versatile - used in web dev, data science, AI, and more
Sarah Tech Senior Developer at Microsoft
987 upvotes

I would recommend JavaScript. It runs in every browser, so you can see results immediately. Plus, you can build both frontend and backend applications with it.

Mike Beginner CS Student
456 upvotes

As someone who just started coding, I found Scratch really helpful for understanding the basics before moving to Python. Don't skip the fundamentals!

Alice Dev Full Stack Developer
78 upvotes

It depends on what you want to do. For web development, start with HTML/CSS then JavaScript. For data science, go with Python. For mobile apps, consider Swift or Kotlin.
